subject: Project Management
title: The Quality Problem
description: A 60-second lesson on The Quality Problem.

hook.text: Before the quality revolution, defects were considered inevitable. Factories expected products to break.

buildup.text: In the early 1900s, manufacturers inspected finished products and threw out the bad ones — waste built into the system.

discovery.text: The cost of poor quality was staggering: rework, scrap, warranty claims, and lost customers.

twist.text: Nobody asked the obvious question: what if we prevented defects instead of finding them?

climax.text: That question launched a revolution that turned quality from inspection into prevention.

punchline.text: Stop finding broken things. Stop making them.

quiz.question: How did early factories handle product defects?
options: Inspected finished products and threw out the bad ones
